article_meta.title: Item 1 of 7 Texas State Troopers line up during a protest at the South Texas Family Residential Center, where Adrian Con

reasoning: The summary inherits some of the article’s framing (“immigration crackdown,” “significant legal rebuke,” “threatening to overwhelm”) and presents it as objective fact. It also omits key countervailing perspectives present in the source (administration statements, appeals court ruling), producing one-sided slant.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses interpretive phrases like “legal rebuke,” “crackdown,” and “threatening to overwhelm” instead of strictly neutral wording.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims mirror the article’s reported findings without explicit attribution but are grounded in the source’s data; minor timeline inaccuracy appears.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Characterizes the rulings as a “significant legal rebuke” in its own voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Slightly heightened language (“surged,” “threatening to overwhelm”) but not overtly alarmist.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assertions about motives or intentions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes court defeats and system strain while downplaying or omitting administration defenses and an appeals court win.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out government justifications, DHS/DOJ statements, and the Fifth Circuit ruling supporting the administration.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Includes “significant” and other evaluative terms that color the narrative.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically charged terms like “crackdown” appear without distancing.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on unlawful detentions, violations, and court burdens more than balancing context.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Relies on court and institutional data without privileging elites over others in a systematic way.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Retains notable elements of the source’s framing instead of neutral paraphrase.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Only lists Reuters; omits perspectives from judges, administration officials, detainees/advocates, and the appeals court.
